completed_since_last_snapshot: PHASE P5 — GDOS SCORING ENGINE IS CLOSED AND VERIFIED (docs/verification/P5.md). S5.8 and S5.9 completed this session; all nine slices S5.1-S5.9 ran the SDLC loop (archives meta/runs/S5.*). Suite 323 -> 336, all green. P5 is the SECOND M2 pillar. MILESTONE M2 STAYS OPEN — only P6 (Campaign Intelligence) remains; the content-generation hard gate is STILL CLOSED.

S5.8 CDRE profile-evolution loop (REQ-052, dm-0042): src/eval/gdos/Cdre.ts. mine(inputs, options) -> CdreProposal[] observes but never mutates, in fixed deterministic order: threshold-adjustment (SYMMETRIC — a metric failing >= failureRateForLowering of reports proposes a LOWER bar at the configured percentile; a metric passing everywhere by >= raiseMarginPoints proposes a RAISE; the raise half is load-bearing, it is what stops a Goodhart ratchet toward vacuous gates), estimator-unmeasurable (a metric scoring 0 in every report is an evidence defect, so no threshold change is proposed), coverage-gap (unexercised axis values), recurring-rejection (a kill reason dominating the record is systemic, not calibration). Guards: cross-profile scores skipped (incomparable, dm-0031); info-density metrics never threshold-mined (band/limit tests, not >= cutoffs); sub-minReports samples mine nothing. apply() accepts ONLY an ACCEPTED threshold-adjustment, is pure, versions profileId (never PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION), and round-trips the result through parseProfile so an invalid evolution fails loudly.

S5.9 verification (REQ-P02): docs/verification/P5.md filed — per-slice proof map, exit-gate evidence, per-REQ verdicts, subtractive pass, ledgered decisions, resolved open questions. NINE REQs flipped to VERIFIED: REQ-020, REQ-021, REQ-040, REQ-042, REQ-051, REQ-052, REQ-055, REQ-056, REQ-111. Ten P5-touching REQs correctly stay IN_PROGRESS because a later phase owns a share: REQ-012/050 (P10), REQ-015 (P10), REQ-016 (P9,P10), REQ-022 (P11), REQ-041 (P6), REQ-053/054/061 (P7).

THE SUBTRACTIVE PASS FOUND REAL DEBT (REQ-022, run WITH the S5.7 engine over all 16 P5 modules): three exports had zero call sites anywhere and were REMOVED — CDRE_BASE_PROFILE (an unused alias for DEFAULT_PROFILE, in Cdre.ts), TICK_SECONDS (an unused re-export of FIXED_STEP_SECONDS, in Evaluate.ts), and runFor (an unused accessor written for a caller that never arrived, in Evidence.ts). After removal the pass re-ran clean (16 held, 16 kept, 0 candidates, 0 incomplete) and the suite stayed 336/336. Recording this matters: a subtraction pass that never removes anything is a rubber stamp.

PROCESS INCIDENT, CONTAINED: an attempt to flip the nine REQs with a shell-quoted regex degenerated (bash escaping collapsed the anchored pattern) and rewrote the FIRST nine 'IN_PROGRESS' occurrences anywhere in docs/requirements_backlog.md — corrupting the States legend and REQ-003/012/014/015/016/022. Caught immediately by diff inspection, reverted with `git checkout -- docs/requirements_backlog.md`, and re-applied with exact-string Edits. Verified after: exactly 9 lines changed, all REQ rows, legend intact. LESSON: never bulk-edit the four-state ledger with a regex through a shell; use exact-string edits and diff-verify.

NEVER bulk-edit docs/requirements_backlog.md (or any four-state ledger) with a regex through a shell — this session's escaping collapsed an anchored pattern and rewrote unrelated rows plus the States legend. Use exact-string Edits, then diff-verify that exactly the intended rows changed. The four-state ledger becoming fiction is the protocol's named anti-pattern.

PARALLEL-SESSION AWARENESS: the user commits directly in this repo between turns (S5.1-S5.4 at cb90d55; S5.5-S5.7 at 3ea8e3a; a toolchain bump to TypeScript ^7.0.2 and their own ledger entry dm-0038 narrowing portal scope to Poki-only, CrazyGames REMOVED not deferred). Check git log/status against this handoff before assuming tree state.

APPEND TO THE LEDGER VIA THE STORE (dm-0040): use dist/src/eval/gdos/DesignMemory.js (parseDesignMemory -> appendDecision -> serializeDesignMemory). Hand-editing risks breaking byte-idempotency, which GdosDesignMemory.test.ts asserts against the LIVE file. Note the store enforces a closed ACCEPTED|REJECTED status set and all five Intent Repository fields, non-empty.

THE GDOS ARCHITECTURE IS SET AND VERIFIED (dm-0031..0042): every score is a proxy over the ONE EvidenceBundle; ALL calibration lives in ScoringProfile (two-profile tests enforce zero calibration literals in gate logic); gates emit DesignDecision records; gdos/ is PURE — it runs no sim, no search, no audit (two scans in EvalIsolation.test.ts). Modules that EXECUTE a sim/search (Evaluate.ts, EmergentFun.ts) live at top-level src/eval/.

PROFILE VERSIONING GRACE IS SPENT (dm-0039): ScoringProfile v1 was extended in place for novelty weights only because no persisted profile existed. Any future field addition bumps PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION with a ledgered migration decision. CDRE's apply() versions profileId, never the schema version.

KNOWN LIMITATION carried to P7 (dm-0041): the REQ-012 kill proxy (a completion tape that never presses THE jump) is necessary but not sufficient — a level where the jump is pressed but irrelevant passes it. The counterfactual no-jump solvability search is deferred to P7 where the search budget already exists.

ONE-WAY DEPENDENCY (dm-0022) + math whitelist (dm-0017) remain scan-enforced across src/eval/ incl. gdos/. No Math.random, no Date.now anywhere in eval (DesignMemory dates and CDRE inputs are parameters).

THE AXIOM: never add a maxJumps field; never re-implement a jump counter. Do NOT change FIXED_STEP_SECONDS (dm-0003); never mutate GameState in place (dm-0004). CANONICAL_PIPELINE from src/eval/AgentHarness.ts is the only sanctioned engine assembly order.
